channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration takes shape: President-elect completes top 15 Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Trump has chosen an ideologically diverse Cabinet for his second term by selecting an array of establishment and unconventional candidates for the top 15 posts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fred Harris, former US senator from Oklahoma and presidential hopeful, dies at 94
item.description: Fred Harris, a former U.S. senator from Oklahoma, presidential hopeful and populist who championed Democratic Party reforms in the turbulent 1960s, died Saturday. He was 94. Harris’ wife, Margaret Elliston, confirmed his death to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Auchincloss: Senate must reject ‘dangerous’ RFK Jr. as HHS head
item.description: Auchincloss’s remarks about RFK echo the concerns of other lawmakers and Kennedy family members alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion groups sue Illinois officials over state insurance law
item.description: The federal lawsuit was filed Wednesday by the Thomas More Society on behalf of six anti-abortion groups.
item.dc:creator: Theodora Koulouvaris, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In deep blue Minneapolis, many Somali voters withheld support for Harris
item.description: According to state elections data, support for Vice President Kamala Harris in parts of Minneapolis with concentrations of East African immigrants and Somali voters was weaker than in the city as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Blinken heads to last G7 meeting of Biden presidency with Ukraine and Mideast topping the agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is leaving on a diplomatic trip to Europe that includes a meeting of foreign ministers from the leading industrialized nations that will focus on the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: 24 states' attorneys general call on Supreme Court to keep biological boys out of girls sports
item.description: Attorneys general from 24 states are asking the Supreme Court to reverse a lower court ruling and uphold an Arizona law to ban biological boys from girls sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An influx of outsiders and money turns Montana Republican, culminating in a Senate triumph
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — Democrats' crushing loss in Montana's nationally important U.S. Senate race settled a fierce political debate over whether a surge of newcomers in the past decade favored Republicans — and if one of the new arrivals could even take high office.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps a Fox News personality, a surgeon and a former Congressman to lead public health agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday nominated Dr. Marty Makary to lead the Food and Drug Administration, selecting a surgeon and author who gained national attention for opposing vaccine mandates and some other public health measures during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: John Hopkins surgeon and COVID contrarian Marty Makary selected to lead Trump’s FDA
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Marty Makary, a Johns Hopkins's surgeon who has espoused contrarian views about the COVID-19 pandemic, to lead the Federal Drug Administration. Makary is a surgical oncologist at Johns Hopkins University and an author.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: State GOP, Dems react to Trump selecting Pam Bondi to be next AG
item.description: Former Florida Attorney General and Temple Terrace native Pam Bondi has been selected to be President-elect Donald Trump’s next U.S. Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance takes on a more visible transition role, working to boost Trump's most contentious picks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After several weeks working mostly behind closed doors, Vice President-elect JD Vance returned to Capitol Hill this week in a new, more visible role: Helping Donald Trump try to get his most contentious Cabinet picks to confirmation in the Senate, where Vance has served for the las
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court takes up another federal agency power challenge
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Friday it will hear a case that could deliver another blow to federal agencies’ powers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Applicants sue Minnesota’s Office of Cannabis Management over disqualification
item.description: The OCM turned to a lottery because more than 1,800 applicants had sought 282 licenses. This week, regulators disqualified two-thirds of the applicants for a variety of reasons.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Feven Gerezgiher, MPR News

item.title: Matt Gaetz says he won't return to Congress next year after withdrawing name for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Rep. Matt Gaetz said Friday that he will not be returning to Congress after withdrawing his name from consideration to be attorney general under President-elect Donald Trump amid growing allegations of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-backed Derek Merrin concedes to Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur in Ohio
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Democratic U.S. Rep. Marcy Kaptur’s Trump-endorsed rival said he called the veteran Ohio congresswoman to concede defeat in the 2024 election. In a video posted to X, formerly Twitter, fourth-term state Rep.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, PIX 11

item.title: Florida officials allege 'pervasive' discrimination against Trump supporters at FEMA
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody and U.S. Rep. Vern Buchanan will hold a news conference Friday "to discuss their efforts to uncover evidence of political discrimination at FEMA and demand accountability," according to a release from Buchanan's office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House passes bill that would allow Treasury to target nonprofits it deems to support terrorism
item.description: The U.S. House passed legislation that would give the Treasury Department unilateral authority to strip the tax-exempt status of nonprofits it claims support terrorism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump names former Florida AG Pam Bondi as his new pick for U.S. attorney general
item.description: After former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration, President-elect Donald Trump named Pam Bondi, a former attorney general of Florida, as his next pick for U.S. attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: How a Northfield farmer picks which of his turkeys to have pardoned at White House
item.description: The competition to meet the president started months ago when John Zimmerman, his son and neighbors began examining 44 turkeys from his flock for the Thanksgiving honor.
item.dc:creator: Todd Melby, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota leads nation in voter turnout, but Wisconsin results loom
item.description: The state canvassing board met in St. Paul Thursday to certify the results of the 2024 election. Wisconsin will certify its results on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
